mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances following an INDPRO decline during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 4.0% triggers immediate discount rate repricing that compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports corporate earnings growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly translates to higher corporate revenue expectations, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates over a 5-year out-of-sample period (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization drops that reduce near-term earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of instances following an INDPRO contraction during late-cycle expansions.
